title: Composition
abstract: The present invention provides a lipid vesicle formed from a lipid bilayer comprising a sphingolipid, a sterol and a phospholipid, and compositions comprising the same. Methods of diagnosing a bacterial infection and/or detecting bacterial colonisation, comprising contacting a sample obtained from a subject with a lipid vesicle of the invention, or a composition of the invention are also provided. Kits comprising at least one swab and lipid vesicles of the invention, or a composition of the invention are also provided.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A lipid vesicle formed from a lipid bilayer comprising a sphingolipid, a sterol and a phospholipid. 2. The lipid vesicle of claim 1, wherein the lipid vesicle is about 50 to 800 nm in diameter. 3. The lipid vesicle of claim 1 or 2, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises 20-80 mol% sphingolipid. 4. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises about 50 mol% sphingolipid. 5. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the sphingolipid is sphingomyelin. 6. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises about 10-40 mol% sterol. 7. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises about 20 mol% sterol. 8. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein sterol is cholesterol. 9. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 8, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises about 0.5-15 mol% phospholipid. 10. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises about 5 mol% phospholipid. 11. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the phospholipid is 1,2- dipalmitoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine (DPPC). 12. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the lipid vesicle further comprises 10,12-tricosadiynoic acid (TCDA). 13. The lipid vesicle of claim 12, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises 10-45 mol% TCDA. 14. The lipid vesicle of claim 12 or 13, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises about 25 mol% TCDA. 15. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 14, wherein the lipid vesicle encapsulates a signalling molecule. 16. The lipid vesicle of claim 15, wherein the signalling molecule is a dye. 17. The lipid vesicle of claim 16, wherein the lipid vesicle comprises the dye at a concentration of about 1-100 mM. 18. The lipid vesicle of claim 16 or 17, wherein the dye selected from one or more of fluorescein, 5(6)-carboxyfluorescein and sulforhodamine B. 19. The lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 17, wherein the lipid vesicle consists essentially of sphingolipid, cholesterol, DPPC and TCDA. 20. A composition comprising lipid vesicles of any of claims 1 to 19. 21. A lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 19 or a composition of claim 20 for use in therapy. 22. A lipid vesicle for use according to claim 21, wherein the lipid vesicle or composition is for use in the diagnosis of a bacterial infection and/or detection of bacterial colonisation. 23. A lipid vesicle for use according to claim 22, wherein the bacterial infection or bacterial colonisation is a Group B Streptococcus infection or colonisation. 24. A method of diagnosing a bacterial infection and/or detecting bacterial colonisation, the method comprising: contacting a sample obtained from a subject with a lipid vesicle of any of claims 1 to 19 or a composition of claim 20; and 

 monitoring for release of the signalling molecule, wherein release of the signalling molecule indicates an infection and/or bacterial colonisation. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein contacting the sample with the lipid vesicle or composition comprises incubating the sample with the lipid vesicle or composition at a temperature of about 30Â°C to about 60Â°C, preferably about 46Â°C. 26. The method of claim 24 or 25, wherein utilising the lipid vesicle or composition with a sample, where the lipid vesicle or composition is at a volume of about 0.1-200pl. 27. The method of any of claims 24 to 26, wherein utilising the lipid vesicle or composition with a sample where the lipid vesicle or composition is diluted with about 0.1-1000pl water soluble solution. 28. The method of any of claims 24 to 27, wherein release of the signalling molecule provides a visible colour change. 29. The method of any of claims 24 to 28, wherein the bacterial infection is a Group B Streptococcus infection. 30. The method of any of claims 24 to 29, wherein the time from contacting the sample with a lipid vesicle to detecting release of the signalling molecule is 90 minutes or less. 31. The method of any of claims 24 to 30, wherein the sample obtained from the patient is an epithelial or mucosal skin swab. 32. The method of claim 31, wherein the mucosal skin swab is a nasal, oral or vaginal swab. 33. A kit comprising (i) at least one swab, and (ii) lipid vesicles of any of claims 1 to 19 or a composition of claim 20.
